stories.title: Trump's deadline for an Iran deal looms. And, Artemis II crew begins the journey home
stories.description: In a press conference last night, Trump reiterated threats against Iran if the country doesn't accept a deal by 8:00 p.m. ET tonight. And, the Artemis II crew are on their way back to Earth.

stories.title: Trump warns 'a whole civilization will die tonight' if Iran fails to meet his deadline
stories.description: With the clock ticking, President Trump radically expanded his threat to the entire Iranian nation if its leaders fail to do a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday night, U.S. Eastern time.

stories.title: Beer cans, helium balloons and mortgages: An unexpected mix of things affected by war
stories.description: It's not just oil and gas that are affected by the Iran war. All sorts of shortages and price spikes are starting to pop up that stand to affect people's daily lives.

stories.title: Trump provides details behind the dramatic rescue of an American airman trapped in Iran
stories.description: President Trump revealed many of the dramatic details on how the U.S. military scrambled to rescue two members of fighter jet that was shot down deep inside Iran.

stories.title: Trump reiterates threats to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges
stories.description: The president has had mixed messages about how and when the U.S.-Israel-led war in Iran will end.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants, bridges. And, Artemis II readies for lunar flyby
stories.description: Trump threatened to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz. And, NASA's Artemis II crew prepares to make its closest approach to the moon.

stories.title: Iran rejects a U.S. ceasefire plan as Trump again threatens to bomb its infrastructure
stories.description: Iran's top officials pushed back against a U.S. ceasefire plan and President Trump's deadline to open the Strait of Hormuz, striking a defiant tone as the warring sides traded missile attacks.

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
stories.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

stories.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
stories.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
stories.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

stories.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
stories.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

stories.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
stories.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
stories.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

stories.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
stories.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Are war crimes being committed in the Middle East war?
stories.description: With US and Israeli strikes on Iran's critical infrastructure already underway Tuesday amid threats of more to come, the UN and other organisations have warned that such attacks could constitute war crimes.

stories.title: An 'eerie still' in Tehran amid Trump's apocalyptic threats
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran for FRANCE 24, Reza Sayah describes an "eerie still" in the Iranian capital ahead of the expiration of the ultimatum set by US President Donald Trump, saying that one "can sense that people are on edge". Trump earlier threatened that a “whole civilization wi...

stories.title: French nationals leave Iran after more than three years in detention
stories.description: Two French nationals, who spent more than three years in an Iranian prison on espionage charges, headed home from the war-stricken country on Tuesday, President Emmanuel Macron said. Cecile Kohler, 41, and Jacques Paris, 72, had been under house arrest at the French embassy in Te...

stories.title: Trump warns a 'whole civilization will die tonight' if a deal with Iran isn't reached
stories.description: U.S. President Donald Trump threatened Tuesday that a “whole civilization will die tonight” if Iran fails to meet his latest deadline to strike a deal that includes reopening the Strait of Hormuz, while the Islamic Republic urged young people to form human chains around power pla...

stories.title: Iran-backed Iraqi armed group release kidnapped US journalist
stories.description: An Iran-backed Iraqi armed group has released an American journalist abducted in Baghdad last week, an Iraqi official said on Tuesday. The group had earlier agreed to release the journalist in a gesture of goodwill toward Iraq’s outgoing prime minister.

stories.title: 🔴 Middle East war live: Russia, China veto UN resolution on reopening Strait of Hormuz
War in the Middle East: Will deal be struck before Trump's deadline?
stories.description: US President Donald Trump warned "a whole civilization will die" in Iran if the country does not heed his midnight cutoff to open the Strait of Hormuz, while Vice President JD Vance said the United States has "tools in our toolkit that we so far haven't decided to use" against Ir...

stories.title: Trump: 'A whole civilisation will die tonight'
stories.description: Airstrikes hit two bridges and a train station in Iran on Tuesday, and Iranian officials urged young people to form human chains to protect power plants, as U.S. President Donald Trump warned that a “whole civilisation will die tonight” if Tehran does not meet his latest deadline...

stories.title: 'Free and on ​their ‌way ⁠to France': Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris released by Iran, says Macron
stories.description: French ​nationals Cécile Kohler ​and Jacques Paris are "free and on ​their ‌way ⁠to France, after ‌three and a half ⁠years of detention in ​Iran", President Emmanuel ‌Macron said Tuesday ‌in a post ​on social media platform X. The two former detainees were released in November af...

stories.title: French nationals return home following Iran prison release
stories.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris were freed in November after more than three years at Evin prison.

stories.title: Iraqi armed group releases abducted US journalist Shelly Kittleson
stories.description: The armed group Kataib Hezbollah said that it would free Kittleson on the condition that she leave Iraq immediately.

stories.title: Tehran synagogue destroyed as US-Israeli strikes kill over a dozen
stories.description: A synagogue in Tehran was struck in a wave of US-Israeli attacks overnight.

stories.title: Why JD Vance joined Pakistan’s last-ditch US-Iran mediation efforts
stories.description: Backchannel talks gain pace as Pakistan mediates and Iran seeks indirect engagement through US vice president.

stories.title: Top university says US-Israel attack targeted Iran’s progress, AI learning
stories.description: Iran condemns systematic attacks on civilian sites, including academic hubs, as war devastates critical infrastructure.

stories.title: US denies nuclear plan as deadline on threat to Iran ‘civilisation’ looms
stories.description: Trump's threat to kill 'whole civilization' and comments from VP have raised fears.

stories.title: Netanyahu: Israel shifting ‘balance of power’ with Iran strikes
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says Israel is intensifying strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Fire erupts near industrial site in Saudi Arabia after Iranian strikes
stories.description: A suspected Iranian missile attack sparked a huge fire near an industrial complex in Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Can Africa tackle the oil shock from the Iran war?
stories.description: African nations are scrambling to secure oil and gas as the Iran war disrupts supplies from the Middle East.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran: ‘A whole civilisation will die tonight’
stories.description: Donald Trump warned that “a whole civilisation will die tonight” if Iran does not open the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: From 9pm shutdowns to remote work: Egypt cuts fuel amid power crisis
stories.description: Egypt is one of the countries implementing energy conservation measures amid the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Trump on Iran: ‘A whole civilisation will die tonight’
stories.description: Increasingly angry US president escalates threats to Iran as his Democratic rivals rebuke him as 'sick' and 'genocidal'.

stories.title: ‘Iranians reject idea that US will bring stability’
stories.description: ‘Iranians reject idea that US will bring stability’

stories.title: Oil production dip in Middle East estimated at 7.5 mln barrels per day — EIA
stories.description: "Oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz continue to be limited causing oil storage to fill quickly in countries that rely on the waterway for exports," the US Energy Information Administration said

stories.title: Iran breaks off direct contacts with US after Trump threatened its destruction — WSJ
stories.description: One source claims that Tehran took the step in a sign of defiance to Washington's position

stories.title: US-Israeli operation against Iran violates UN charter — China’s representative
stories.description: The sovereignty, security and the territorial integrity of the Gulf States must be fully respected, Fu Cong said

stories.title: Ex-chief of US counterterrorism center warns US against attempts to destroy Iran
stories.description: There is still time to avert disaster if US President Donald Trump finds the courage to pursue serious negotiations rather than reckless rage and destruction, Joe Kent said

stories.title: Rubio hopes to receive new information about possibility of US-Iran deal
stories.description: The head of the US foreign policy department noted that, as a result of the conflict with Iran, "the whole world’s been impacted" with economic consequences

stories.title: If Trump implements his threat against Iran, this would be gravest crime — UN
stories.description: "Threats that spread fear and terror among civilians are unacceptable and must cease immediately," Volker Turk said

stories.title: US leader not banking on deal with Iran — interview
stories.description: Donald Trump said that he didn’t want to put odds on it

stories.title: Bahrain's resolution on the Strait of Hormuz was not adopted by the UN Security Council
stories.description: Russia and China opposed the measure

stories.title: United States eyeing control over Iranian oil as method of pressure on China — Bloomberg
stories.description: According to the report, the administration in Washington is discussing the weakening of Chinese positions as a result of US operations in Venezuela and the Middle East

stories.title: Military command hands Trump new options for strikes on Iran — newspaper
stories.description: According to the report, the US military is actively preparing to enter a new phase of its operation against Iran

stories.title: Iran war close to over, Kiev’s interference in elections: Vance’s statements
stories.description: The US vice president said that reaching peace in the shortest order meets the interests of the United States, Europe and Ukraine

stories.title: Bridge in Iran’s Mianeh destroyed after US-Israeli attack — TV
stories.description: No further details were provided

stories.title: Iran releases two French spies after UN lawsuit withdrawn - IRNA
stories.description: The French authorities also released Iranian translator Mahdieh Esfandiari, who was detained in 2025 for allegedly justifying terrorism

stories.title: Vice president says US military hits military targets on Iran’s Kharg Island
stories.description: According to JD Vance, the strikes had nothing to do with US President Donald Trump’s deadline for a deal with Tehran

stories.title: Netanyahu confirms Israeli airstrikes on Iranian railways and bridges
stories.description: As the Israeli prime minister said, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had used railways and bridges to deliver materials for producing armaments and deploying weapons and troops

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
